introduction: An abnormal and uncomfortable awareness of breathing Effort of breathing is out of proportion with exertion needs Patients often have difficulties in describing the discomfort of dyspnoea

features: Will depend on the underlying cause(s) of dyspnoea

non_drug: Other treatment will depend on the underlying/ precipitating cause

differential_diagnoses: Obstructive airways disease: asthma, chronic bronchitis, emphysema
Parenchymal lung disease: pneumonia, pneumoconiosis, pulmonary fibrosis
Chest wall disorders: respiratory muscle paralysis, kyphoscoliosis
